title: Tanium Real-Time Question Response Times
spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype="tanium:interact"
| stats perc95(response_ms) as p95 by question_name
| where p95>5000
description: Response times matter when Tanium is used for incident containment questions.
value: Response times matter when Tanium is used for incident containment questions.
implementation: Monitor Interact latency for live investigations. Alert when platform or network degrades.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Performance.CPU by Performance.host | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Tanium baselines and question results may show drift right after software deployments, OS patches, or approved configuration baselines; pair with change and endpoint ownership before filing defects.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_tanium`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=tanium:interact`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Monitor Interact latency for live investigations. Alert when platform or network degrades.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype="tanium:interact"
| stats perc95(response_ms) as p95 by question_name
| where p95>5000
```

Understanding this SPL

**Tanium Real-Time Question Response Times** ‚Äî Response times matter when Tanium is used for incident containment questions.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=tanium:interact`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_tanium`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: endpoint; **sourcetype**: tanium:interact. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: Anomaly ‚Äî interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=endpoint, sourcetype="tanium:interact". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by question_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where p95>5000` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Performance.CPU by Performance.host | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Tanium Real-Time Question Response Times** ‚Äî Response times matter when Tanium is used for incident containment questions.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=tanium:interact`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_tanium`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Detection type** for this use case: Anomaly ‚Äî interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Performance.CPU` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Run the search over a window that includes both normal production traffic and, if you have one, a documented test (vendor sandbox, lab rule hit, or exercise). Compare a few sample events to the vendor‚Äôs own console or reports and tune indexes, fields, and thresholds so alerts are actionable before paging.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (p95 latency), Table (slow questions).
grandmaExplanation: We use Tanium data and alerts to stay ahead of the risks described in "Tanium Real-Time Question Response Times". We help the team notice trouble early, while a situation is still small and containable.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Performance.CPU` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
